name: Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail
name_original: Comarca del Matarranya – de Vall de Roures a Beseit
description: Cycle an easy 10.4-mile (16.8 km) touring route from Valderrobres to Beceite in the scenic Comarca del Matarranya region.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valderrobres traverse a landscape characterized by ancient olive and almond groves, vineyards, and the Matarraña River. The region, situated at an elevation of 500 meters above sea level, offers diverse terrain from river valleys to the edge of Els Ports Natural Park. Cyclists encounter varied physical features, including natural swimming pools, reservoirs, and distinctive rock formations. The area provides a network of routes suitable for different skill levels, from flat greenways to more challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Valderrobres The most popular touring cycling route is Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail, offering a gentle ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pantano de Pena – Beceite Stone Bridge loop from El Roble, a moderate 15.5 miles (24.9 km) path. This route includes views of the Pantano de Pena reservoir and the historic Beceite Stone Bridge. Local touring cyclists also love the Greenway – Beceite Stone Bridge loop from Antigua Estación de Valderobres, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including sections of the Vía Verde, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Valderrobres is defined by the Matarraña River, extensive olive and almond groves, and the mountainous edges of Els Ports Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more demanding climbs. The routes in Valderrobres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valderrobres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Valderrobres?
A: Valderrobres, often called 'The Tuscany of Spain,' offers diverse terrain. You'll cycle through ancient olive and almond groves, vineyards, and along the Matarraña River. Routes range from gentle river valleys to the mountainous edges of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, providing varied landscapes and elevations.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Valderrobres?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Valderrobres, catering to various skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 21 more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Valderrobres?
A: Yes, Valderrobres offers several easy routes perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956740902/matarrana-algars-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.4-mile (16.8 km) path that follows a gentle gradient through picturesque landscapes. The Vía Verde (Greenway) also provides largely flat, relaxed cycling through tunnels and open countryside.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Valderrobres has 21 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valldeltormo Station – Railway Tunnel loop from Antigua Estación de Valderobres&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65.5 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the scenic Matarraña River, natural swimming pools, and reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the breathtaking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; canyon or the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Valderrobres itself is a designated Historic Site. You can explore the charming medieval old town, the monumental Valderrobres Castle, and the Gothic Santa María la Mayor Church. The iconic Stone Bridge over the Matarraña River is also a notable landmark. Nearby villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1563297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite&lt;/a&gt; also offer cultural insights.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Valderrobres?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde (Greenway) is an excellent choice for families, offering a largely flat and safe path through scenic countryside. Many of the easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956740902/matarrana-algars-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are also suitable for families looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Valderrobres are designed as loops. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Pena – Beceite Stone Bridge loop from El Roble&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the reservoir and historic bridge. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31486817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway – Beceite Stone Bridge loop from Antigua Estación de Valderobres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Valderrobres?
A: Valderrobres, situated at 500 meters above sea level, enjoys a more temperate climate than coastal regions. This makes it ideal for outdoor pursuits, including touring cycling, even during the summer months. Spring and autumn also offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes.

Q: Can I see wildlife while cycling in Valderrobres?
A: The region is home to diverse wildlife, especially around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Cyclists might spot ibex, foxes, wild boars, and various bird species, including vultures. The extensive pine forests and varied ecosystems provide excellent opportunities for wildlife observation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Valderrobres?
A: The touring cycling routes in Valderrobres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse and unspoiled natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of challenging climbs with relaxed greenways.

Q: Is it possible to cycle with dogs on the routes in Valderrobres?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for each route or natural park, especially within &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Generally, dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure safety for all users.
tips: The church of Santa María is one of the greatest exponents of Gothic architecture in Aragon and is connected to the Valderrobres castle-palace, which is also Gothic.
This river, a tributary of the Matarranya, crosses the border between Aragon and Catalonia. In this area it is possible to bathe in its waters.

Baceite is a Spanish municipality and town in the province of Teruel located in the Matarraña region that offers us a wide variety of routes, natural and cultural heritage, or visits to museums and/or leisure centers. We can find more information at the tourist office or the website https://www.beceite.es/

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This easy touring bicycle route is approximately 16.8 km long and generally takes about 1 hour and 5 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts in Valderrobres (Vall de Roures) and leads towards Beceite (Beseit). Valderrobres, being a significant town, offers various parking options, including public parking areas near the historic center. You can usually find parking close to the starting points within the town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Comarca del Matarranya region, often called 'Spanish Tuscany,' is beautiful year-round, but the best times for cycling are spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant with blooming flowers or autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on cycling routes in the Matarranya region, especially on public paths and quiet roads. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, particularly when passing through villages or near livestock. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this specific touring bicycle route between Valderrobres and Beceite. The route uses public roads and established paths.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: This route offers an easy touring bicycle experience, primarily on a mix of good tracks, quiet roads, and possibly some well-maintained dirt trails. While the broader Matarranya region has varied terrain, this specific route is designed to be accessible and enjoyable for most cyclists.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route from Valderrobres to Beceite?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich blend of natural beauty and historical charm. Highlights include the historic town center and stone bridge of Valderrobres, its impressive Castle and Church, and the scenic Río Ulldemó. The route culminates in the picturesque settlement of Beceite, with views of olive groves, almond trees, and riverside forests.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several established paths. Notably, about 50% of it follows the Camino Natural del Matarraña - Algars. You'll also find it intersects with parts of Pedals dels Ports and El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, offering connections to broader cycling networks in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the way?
faqs.answer: Both Valderrobres and Beceite are charming towns with various cafes, restaurants, and local shops where you can refuel and enjoy local cuisine. While the route itself might not have many direct stops, you'll find plenty of options at the start and end points.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, given its 'easy' difficulty rating and relatively short distance, this route is generally suitable for families with children who are comfortable cycling. The terrain is manageable, and the scenic views make for an enjoyable outing. For an even more family-friendly experience, parts of the nearby Vía Verde del Valle de Zafán are known for being very accessible.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy touring route, we recommend bringing water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), a basic bike repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Comfortable cycling attire and appropriate footwear are also essential. Depending on the season, a light jacket might be useful for cooler mornings or evenings.

waypoints.name: Valderrobres — Historic Town Center and Stone Bridge
